channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 137 orthopedic patients found that an advanced virtual physical therapy model achieved a 70.9% care completion rate, exceeding national benchmarks, with 96% of completers achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences in functional improvements.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: Combining gait, balance, and infrared thermography data significantly enhances machine-learning classification of knee osteoarthritis (KOA) compared to single-domain models, improving discrimination between KOA patients and healthy controls.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) alone for muscle strength, pain, and disability in musculoskeletal conditions, though direct comparison to high-load exercise (HLE) was also evaluated.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled study found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared to senior physicians for musculoskeletal conditions in an emergency department setting.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study involving 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults compared the accuracy of stride count estimation across multiple wearable sensor configurations (wrists, hips, ankles) and algorithms against a gold-standard instrumented treadmill.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: Researchers developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into functioning, distress, and context domains, suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis of 62,217 Medicare beneficiaries in the US found that the mandatory Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) bundled payment model significantly reduced the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials with EDSS scores ≤6.0.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: A follow-up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries provides an updated vision of care, examining recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prehabilitation Improves Surgical Outcomes in Older Adults Undergoing Spinal Fusion: Study
item.description: A randomized clinical trial found that a multimodal prehabilitation program before spinal fusion surgery in older adults was associated with lower postoperative complications and shorter hospital stays compared to standard care, though control group patients showed better physical function at 90 days.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissue: guidelines from the American Society of Regional Anesthesia and Pain Medicine, the American Academy of Pain Medicine, the American Society of Interventional Pain Physicians, and the International Pain and Spine Intervention Society
item.description: New guidelines from multiple American pain societies address the use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissues, highlighting that recommended effective doses are lower than currently used by clinicians and emphasizing short-term benefits with mixed long-term relief.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Immediate Weight-Bearing and Early Ambulation Rehabilitation After Femoral Neck System Fixation for Undisplaced Femoral Neck Fractures: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: A prospective randomized controlled trial investigated immediate weight-bearing and early ambulation rehabilitation after femoral neck system fixation for undisplaced femoral neck fractures.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis for the female, woman and girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis on prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury in female athletes, contributing to the Female, Woman and Girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The Impact of a Single Bout of Antagonist Stretching on Agonist Muscle Performance and Range of Motion: A Systematic review and Meta-analysis
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis investigated the impact of a single bout of antagonist stretching on agonist muscle performance and range of motion, aiming to synthesize conflicting findings.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review identified gaps in rehabilitation strategies for chronic stroke upper limb impairment, comparing outcomes and protocols between patients with and without contralateral C7 (CC7) nerve transfer and technology-assisted rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A multi-method study, combining an in-person summit and a Delphi consensus process with 83 participants from various tennis governing bodies and the broader tennis ecosystem, generated recommendations for mental healthcare and mental wellness promotion among elite and junior tennis athletes.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Preoperative physiotherapy and one-year patient-reported outcomes after primary total knee arthroplasty: a registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients.
item.description: A registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients found no significant difference in KOOS-PS at 2 months and 1 year post-TKA between those who received preoperative physiotherapy and those who did not.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Short-term effects of therapeutic massage alone or combined with conventional physiotherapy in myofascial pain syndrome: A randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial with 60 participants with upper trapezius MPS found that therapeutic massage alone or combined with conventional physiotherapy showed short-term effects on pain, pressure pain threshold, muscle activity, functional disability, and quality of life.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The WalkBack (walking and education) program for prevention of low back pain is acceptable to physiotherapists but relies on training, flexibility and system-level enablers to optimise implementation: a qualitative study.
item.description: A qualitative study of 20 Australian physiotherapists found that the WalkBack (walking and education) program for low back pain prevention is acceptable, but its implementation relies on training, flexibility, and system-level enablers to optimize adoption in routine practice.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Profile of patients attending private vestibular physiotherapy clinics in Spain: A cross-sectional study.
item.description: A multicenter cross-sectional study in Spain characterized the demographic, clinical, and healthcare utilization profiles of patients attending private vestibular physiotherapy clinics, aiming to understand access and referral limitations.
item.author: PubMed
